name: Get-ChildItem
synopsis: Gets items and child items in one or more locations.
description: Retrieves files, folders, registry keys, certificates, and other items from any PowerShell provider location. Use -Recurse to descend into subdirectories and -Filter for fast wildcard filtering at the provider level.

examples.description: List files in the current directory

name: Set-Location
synopsis: Sets the current working location to a specified location.
description: Changes the current working directory or registry hive. Equivalent to the 'cd' command in most shells.

name: Get-Location
synopsis: Gets information about the current working location or a location stack.
description: Returns the path of the current working directory. The output object can be stored and used in scripts.

examples.description: Store current path in a variable

name: New-Item
synopsis: Creates a new item such as a file, directory, or symbolic link.
description: Creates files, directories, symbolic links, registry keys/values, and other items depending on the active provider.

name: Remove-Item
synopsis: Deletes the specified items.
description: Permanently deletes files, directories, registry keys, and other items. Use -Recurse to delete directories and their contents, and -Force to remove read-only items.

examples.description: Delete a folder and all contents

name: Copy-Item
synopsis: Copies an item from one location to another.
description: Copies files, directories, and other items to a new location. Works across providers and supports -Recurse for directories.

name: Move-Item
synopsis: Moves an item from one location to another.
description: Moves files, directories, or other items and optionally renames them in the process.

name: Rename-Item
synopsis: Renames an item in a PowerShell provider namespace.
description: Renames a single file, directory, or other item in place without moving it.

name: Get-Content
synopsis: Gets the content of the item at the specified location.
description: Reads a file line by line (or in raw mode as a single string). Supports -Tail for the last N lines, similar to tail -f on Unix.

name: Set-Content
synopsis: Writes or replaces the content in an item with new content.
description: Overwrites the contents of a file with new content. Unlike Add-Content, it replaces rather than appends.

name: Add-Content
synopsis: Adds content to the specified items, such as adding words to a file.
description: Appends content to an existing file without overwriting. If the file does not exist, it is created.

examples.description: Append a line to a log file

name: Test-Path
synopsis: Determines whether all elements of a path exist.
description: Returns $true if the specified path exists, $false otherwise. Use -PathType to distinguish files from containers.

name: Get-Item
synopsis: Gets the item at the specified location.
description: Returns the item at a given path (file, directory, registry key, etc.) as an object without enumerating its children.

name: Get-Process
synopsis: Gets the processes that are running on the local computer.
description: Returns process objects with information about CPU, memory, and other resource usage. Supports filtering by name, ID, or remote computer.

name: Stop-Process
synopsis: Stops one or more running processes.
description: Terminates processes by name or ID. Use -Force to kill unresponsive processes. -WhatIf previews without acting.

name: Start-Process
synopsis: Starts one or more processes on the local computer.
description: Launches an application or script. Supports running as administrator (-Verb RunAs), waiting for completion (-Wait), and capturing the process object (-PassThru).

name: Wait-Process
synopsis: Waits for the processes to be stopped before accepting more input.
description: Blocks execution until the named or specified process exits, optionally with a timeout in seconds.

name: Test-Connection
synopsis: Sends ICMP echo requests to one or more computers.
description: Pings one or more hosts and returns result objects with round-trip time and status. -Quiet returns a boolean for use in conditional logic.

name: Test-NetConnection
synopsis: Displays diagnostic information for a connection.
description: Tests TCP port connectivity, performs traceroute, and displays network interface and route information. More powerful than Test-Connection.

examples.description: Test HTTPS port on a website

name: Get-NetIPConfiguration
synopsis: Gets IP network configuration.
description: Returns IP address, default gateway, and DNS server configuration for each network adapter.

name: Get-NetIPAddress
synopsis: Gets the IP address configuration.
description: Returns all configured IP addresses on all network interfaces, including IPv4 and IPv6.

name: Resolve-DnsName
synopsis: Performs a DNS name query resolution.
description: Performs DNS lookups for A, AAAA, MX, TXT, CNAME, and other record types. Replaces the legacy nslookup tool.

name: Get-NetTCPConnection
synopsis: Gets current TCP connections.
description: Returns all active and listening TCP connections, similar to netstat. Filter by state such as Listen, Established, or TimeWait.

name: Get-ComputerInfo
synopsis: Gets a consolidated object of system and operating system properties.
description: Returns a rich object containing OS version, hardware, BIOS, memory, CPU, and Windows features in one call.

name: Get-PSDrive
synopsis: Gets drives in the current session.
description: Lists all PowerShell drives, including filesystem drives, the registry, environment variables, certificates, and functions.

name: Get-EventLog
synopsis: Gets the events in an event log on local or remote computers.
description: Reads Windows Event Log entries. Use -LogName 'System', 'Application', or 'Security'. Deprecated in favor of Get-WinEvent on newer systems.

name: Get-WinEvent
synopsis: Gets events from event logs and event tracing log files.
description: Modern replacement for Get-EventLog. Supports ETL files, XML filtering, and works on all Windows event logs including forwarded events.

name: Get-Service
synopsis: Gets the services on a computer.
description: Returns Windows service objects. Filter by name, display name, or status. Can query remote computers.

name: Start-Service
synopsis: Starts one or more stopped services.
description: Starts a stopped Windows service. Requires administrative privileges. Use Get-Service to check status first.

examples.description: Start and get the service object

name: Stop-Service
synopsis: Stops one or more running services.
description: Stops a running Windows service. -Force stops dependent services automatically.

name: Restart-Service
synopsis: Stops and then starts one or more services.
description: Stops and immediately restarts a service. Equivalent to Stop-Service followed by Start-Service.

name: Get-Variable
synopsis: Gets the variables in the current console.
description: Returns variable objects from the current session or a specified scope (Global, Script, Local).

name: Set-Variable
synopsis: Sets the value of a variable. Creates the variable if one with the requested name does not exist.
description: Creates or updates a variable. Use -Option ReadOnly or Constant to prevent modification.

name: Remove-Variable
synopsis: Deletes a variable and its value.
description: Removes the variable from the current scope. Use -Force to remove read-only variables.

name: Write-Host
synopsis: Writes customized output to the host (console).
description: Writes text directly to the console with optional color. NOTE: output cannot be captured via the pipeline; use Write-Output for that.

name: Write-Output
synopsis: Sends the specified objects to the next command in the pipeline.
description: Places objects into the pipeline. Unlike Write-Host, the output CAN be captured by variables or piped to other commands.

name: Write-Error
synopsis: Writes an object to the error stream.
description: Generates a non-terminating error and writes it to the error stream. Does not stop script execution by default.

name: Write-Warning
synopsis: Writes a warning message.
description: Outputs a yellow-colored warning message to the warning stream. Can be suppressed with -WarningAction SilentlyContinue.

name: Write-Verbose
synopsis: Writes text to the verbose message stream.
description: Outputs diagnostic messages only when -Verbose is specified or $VerbosePreference = 'Continue'. Ideal for debugging.

name: Write-Debug
synopsis: Writes a debug message to the console.
description: Outputs messages only when the debug stream is enabled via -Debug or $DebugPreference = 'Continue'.

name: Format-Table
synopsis: Formats the output as a table.
description: Displays objects in a tabular format. Use -AutoSize to fit column widths, and -Property to select specific properties.

name: Format-List
synopsis: Formats the output as a list of properties in which each property appears on a new line.
description: Displays each object property on its own line, useful for wide or deeply nested objects.

name: Select-Object
synopsis: Selects objects or object properties.
description: Filters properties, picks the first/last N objects, or expands a single property from pipeline objects.

name: Where-Object
synopsis: Selects objects from a collection based on their property values.
description: Filters pipeline objects by property comparison or script block. Equivalent to SQL WHERE. The alias ? is commonly used.

name: Sort-Object
synopsis: Sorts objects by property values.
description: Sorts pipeline objects by one or more properties in ascending or descending order.

name: Group-Object
synopsis: Groups objects that contain the same value for specified properties.
description: Groups pipeline objects by a shared property value and returns count, name, and group members.

name: Measure-Object
synopsis: Calculates the numeric properties of objects, and the characters, words, and lines in string objects.
description: Performs aggregate calculations. Combine with -Sum, -Average, -Maximum, -Minimum for numeric data or -Word/-Line for text.

name: ForEach-Object
synopsis: Performs an operation against each item in a collection of input objects.
description: Iterates over each pipeline object and runs a script block. Inside the block, $_ or $PSItem refers to the current object.

name: Invoke-Command
synopsis: Runs commands on local and remote computers.
description: Executes a script block locally or on one or more remote computers via WinRM. Supports existing sessions (-Session) and background jobs (-AsJob).

examples.description: Run locally in a new scope

name: New-PSSession
synopsis: Creates a persistent connection to a local or remote computer.
description: Opens a reusable PowerShell remoting session. Persistent sessions are more efficient than creating a new connection per command.

name: Import-Module
synopsis: Adds modules to the current session.
description: Loads a module's cmdlets, functions, variables, and providers into the current session. Supports module names or paths.

name: Get-Module
synopsis: Gets the modules that have been imported or that can be imported into the current session.
description: Lists loaded modules (-All for all) or all modules installed on the system (-ListAvailable).

examples.description: Check if a module is available

name: Install-Module
synopsis: Downloads and installs one or more modules from a repository.
description: Installs modules from the PowerShell Gallery or another registered repository. Use -Scope CurrentUser to install without admin rights.

name: Get-Command
synopsis: Gets all commands that are installed on the computer.
description: Discovers cmdlets, functions, scripts, and aliases. Use -Verb and -Noun to find related commands or -Module to scope to a module.

name: Get-Help
synopsis: Displays information about PowerShell commands and concepts.
description: Displays built-in documentation for any cmdlet, function, or concept. Use Update-Help first to download the latest help files.

name: Get-Member
synopsis: Gets the properties and methods of objects.
description: Reveals the type, properties, methods, and events of any object. Essential for exploring unfamiliar objects in the pipeline.

name: Invoke-RestMethod
synopsis: Sends an HTTP or HTTPS request to a RESTful web service.
description: Makes HTTP requests and automatically parses JSON, XML, or CSV responses into PowerShell objects. The go-to tool for API calls.

name: Invoke-WebRequest
synopsis: Gets content from a web page on the internet.
description: Downloads web pages and returns the full response including headers, status code, and parsed HTML. For raw HTTP work, prefer this over Invoke-RestMethod.

name: ConvertTo-Json
synopsis: Converts an object to a JSON-formatted string.
description: Serializes PowerShell objects to JSON. Use -Depth to control how deeply nested objects are serialized (default is 2).

name: ConvertFrom-Json
synopsis: Converts a JSON-formatted string to a custom object or hash table.
description: Deserializes a JSON string into a PowerShell PSCustomObject or (with -AsHashtable) a hashtable.

name: Export-Csv
synopsis: Converts objects into a series of comma-separated value (CSV) strings and saves the strings to a file.
description: Serializes objects to CSV format. Each property becomes a column header. Use -NoTypeInformation to omit the #TYPE line.

name: Import-Csv
synopsis: Creates table-like custom objects from the items in a CSV file.
description: Reads a CSV file and returns one object per row, with property names from the header row.

name: Get-Date
synopsis: Gets the current date and time.
description: Returns a DateTime object for the current or specified date/time. Use -Format for .NET format strings or -UFormat for Unix-style.

name: Start-Sleep
synopsis: Suspends the activity in a script or session for the specified period of time.
description: Pauses script execution for a given number of seconds or milliseconds.

name: Clear-Host
synopsis: Clears the display in the host program.
description: Clears the console terminal screen.

name: Read-Host
synopsis: Reads a line of input from the console.
description: Prompts the user for input. Use -AsSecureString for passwords to avoid storing them in plain text.

name: Tee-Object
synopsis: Saves command output in a file or variable and also sends it down the pipeline.
description: Splits the pipeline stream so output goes to both a file/variable and continues down the pipeline.

name: Out-File
synopsis: Sends output to a file.
description: Redirects pipeline output to a file. Unlike Export-Csv or ConvertTo-Json, it writes the formatted text representation.

name: Out-GridView
synopsis: Sends output to an interactive table in a separate window.
description: Opens an interactive GUI grid that allows sorting, filtering, and selecting rows. Use -PassThru to return selected items to the pipeline.

name: Select-String
synopsis: Finds text in strings and files.
description: Searches strings and files using regular expressions. Returns match objects with filename, line number, and matched text. Equivalent to grep.

examples.description: Search for a pattern in files

name: Get-Credential
synopsis: Gets a credential object based on a user name and password.
description: Prompts for username and password and returns a PSCredential object. Credentials are stored securely and never as plain-text strings.

name: ConvertTo-SecureString
synopsis: Converts plain text or an encrypted standard string to a secure string.
description: Creates a SecureString from plain text. In scripts, use -AsPlainText -Force. For production, prefer encrypted strings saved with ConvertFrom-SecureString.

name: Set-ExecutionPolicy
synopsis: Sets the PowerShell execution policy for Windows computers.
description: Controls which scripts are allowed to run. Policies: Restricted, AllSigned, RemoteSigned, Unrestricted, Bypass. Use -Scope to limit the change.

name: Get-ScheduledTask
synopsis: Gets the task definition object of a scheduled task.
description: Returns scheduled task objects. Filter by task name or path. Works on local and remote computers.

name: Register-ScheduledTask
synopsis: Registers a scheduled task definition on a local computer.
description: Creates a new scheduled task using an action and trigger. Build the action with New-ScheduledTaskAction and the trigger with New-ScheduledTaskTrigger.

name: Get-ItemProperty
synopsis: Gets the properties of a specified item.
description: Reads registry values when used with the HKLM: or HKCU: provider. Also works on file/folder attributes.

examples.description: Read all values in a key

name: Set-ItemProperty
synopsis: Creates or changes the value of a property of an item.
description: Writes registry values or file attributes. Use -Type to specify registry value type: String, DWord, QWord, ExpandString, Binary, MultiString.
